fallback.problem: {name} targets a clear need in its sector. Review the summary and documents before investing.
fallback.solution: The campaign describes how funds will be used to deliver outcomes. Confirm assumptions with the team before investing.
fallback.team: Core team bios and roles will appear here when the innovator completes their profile.
fallback.risksDisclosure: All crowdfunding involves risk of partial or total loss. Read all materials carefully.
fallback.risksLevelExpl: Risk level is self-reported and not investment advice.
fallback.risksConsiderations: Diversify, invest only what you can afford to lose, and seek professional advice if unsure.
fallback.updateBody: The innovator has not yet posted a detailed timeline of updates for this listing.

solar-dryer-farmers.problem: Smallholders lose 15–30% of harvest to spoilage when rain hits during open-air drying. Diesel dryers are costly and fuel supply is unreliable.
solar-dryer-farmers.solution: We manufacture modular solar tunnel dryers with local sheet metal and polycarbonate. Cooperatives share units across a harvest calendar, cutting moisture to safe storage levels within 48 hours.
solar-dryer-farmers.team: John Doe — founder & agronomy lead (10 yrs extension work). Mulugeta A. — mechanical design. Sara K. — cooperative partnerships and training.
solar-dryer-farmers.risksDisclosure: Market adoption may be slower if cooperatives prioritize other capital needs. Supply chain delays could push pilot dates. Currency and input cost volatility may affect margins.
solar-dryer-farmers.riskLevelExplanation: Technology is proven at pilot scale but field deployment and payment behavior across cooperatives remain uncertain.
solar-dryer-farmers.investorConsiderations: Suitable for investors comfortable with hardware rollout risk and 2–3 year impact horizons. Not a liquid investment.

updates.title: Pilot dryers performing well
updates.body: Moisture readings improved versus open-air drying; farmer feedback incorporated into rack spacing.

updates.title: Materials secured
updates.body: Local glazing and metal stock arrived on budget; second assembly line starting this week.

updates.title: Campaign live
updates.body: Thank you for backing us — we will post specs and photos as milestones complete.

qa.q: What warranty do you offer on the tunnel frame?
qa.a: Five years on structural welds against manufacturing defect; consumables excluded.
qa.author: Daniel T.

qa.q: Can one dryer serve more than one cooperative per season?
qa.a: Yes — we schedule non-overlapping windows; typical utilization is 3–4 groups per year.
qa.author: Hanna K.

qa.a: Two-day on-site training is included per pilot site; refresher videos in Amharic and Oromo.
qa.author: Anonymous investor

initial.title: Pilot dryers performing well
paragraphs: Moisture readings are down 40% versus open-air drying in the first cooperative. Farmers reported fewer spoiled crops during recent rain.
We are scheduling maintenance training for next week and refining the rack layout based on feedback.

initial.title: Materials secured
paragraphs: Local sheet metal and glazing arrived on budget. Assembly of the second pilot unit starts this week.

initial.title: Campaign launched
paragraphs: Thank you to everyone who backed us so far. We will post technical specs and photos as milestones complete.
